item.title: Run Google Chrome as non root user in Kali Linux
item.description: This post is a part of the Project Restore, with the primary aim of excavating my previous writings. The material contained herein may not hold practical relevance and is most likely completely useless. This post is about running Google’s Chrome browser in Kali linux as a no-root user. Before I get into it, I would like to say that you can run Chrome as root and there are plenty of tutorials showing you how to do that.

item.title: Connect to ssh server on Kali Linux
item.description: For some of my work I run Kali in Virtual Box and I was getting a bit tired of switching between different windows on my mac host. So I thought I will ssh into my Kali box and enjoy the iTerm eye-candy. The version of Kali I have running which is 1.07 has openssh server installed but the service is not running by default. To run the service I ran this command
